intro_mechanics.force_implies_motion_direct: A book lies at rest on a table. What is the net force on the book? Explain your reasoning in one or two sentences.
intro_mechanics.force_implies_motion_scenario: A car moves at constant speed along a straight road. Is there a net force on the car? Why or why not?
intro_mechanics.velocity_implies_force_direct: An object is moving to the right. Does there have to be a force acting on it to the right? Explain.
intro_mechanics.action_reaction_cancel_direct: When you push on a wall, the wall pushes back. Why don't these two forces cancel so that nothing moves?
intro_mechanics.action_reaction_cancel_scenario: A horse pulls a cart. The cart pulls back on the horse. Why can the cart still accelerate forward?
intro_mechanics.friction_direction_direct: A block slides to the right on a rough floor. In what direction does friction act on the block? Justify briefly.
intro_mechanics.n2_mass_inverse_direct: Two objects experience the same net force. Object A has greater mass than Object B. Which has the greater acceleration? Explain.
intro_mechanics.projectile_no_force_horizontal: A ball is thrown horizontally. After it leaves the hand, is there a horizontal force on the ball? What about vertical? Explain.
intro_mechanics.circular_centripetal_direction: A ball on a string is swung in a horizontal circle. When the string breaks, in what direction does the ball fly? Why?
intro_mechanics.energy_work_sign: You slow down a moving block by pushing against its motion. Is the work you do on the block positive, negative, or zero? Explain.
intro_mechanics.momentum_conservation_isolated: Two skaters push off each other on frictionless ice. How do their velocities after the push compare to before? Use momentum in your answer.
